codeReview.summary: Comprehensive CLI skeleton implementation with clap derive. All expected behaviors from the feature specification are correctly implemented: Parser struct with global --json and --no-color flags, 14 subcommand variants, workspace resolution walking up from CWD with ~/.werk/ fallback, ID prefix resolution with 4+ char minimum and case-insensitive matching, output formatting module supporting human/JSON modes with color control via owo-colors, and error types with thiserror providing correct exit codes (0/1/2). The code includes #![forbid(unsafe_code)] at crate root as required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed AGENTS.md conventions: clap derive macros, exit codes 0/1/2, NO_COLOR support, ID prefix minimum 4 chars, tempfile for tests. All patterns are now properly documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/src/main.rs:1-10 - #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and exit code documentation; werk-cli/src/output.rs:37-42 - NO_COLOR env var check; werk-cli/src/prefix.rs:12 - MIN_PREFIX_LEN constant

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed rust-cli-worker SKILL.md procedure precisely: wrote tests first (RED), implemented to make tests pass (GREEN), ran lint/format, did manual verification, committed with descriptive message.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 25 unit tests + 7 integration tests added before/during implementation; verification section shows all 4 required commands (test, clippy, fmt, sd-core regression) were run

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker updated .factory/services.yaml with werk-specific commands (test_werk, clippy_werk, run_werk) and changed existing commands to --workspace scope. This was necessary as the project expanded from sd-core-only to a workspace with werk-cli.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml now includes test_werk, clippy_werk, run_werk commands and uses --workspace for build/test/clippy/fmt

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker documented werk-cli architecture in .factory/library/architecture.md, including module structure and key patterns. This knowledge capture will help future workers understand the CLI architecture.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md lines 56-83 contain new werk-cli section with module structure and patterns

summary: foundation-skeleton is a complete, high-quality implementation. All 14 subcommands are defined with clap derive, global --json and --no-color flags work correctly, workspace resolution walks up from CWD with ~/.werk/ fallback, ID prefix resolution handles 4+ char case-insensitive matching with ambiguity detection, output formatting supports human/JSON modes with proper color control, and error types use thiserror with correct exit codes (0/1/2). 32 total tests pass (25 unit + 7 integration), clippy reports zero warnings, and #![forbid(unsafe_code)] is present at crate root. Worker followed the rust-cli-worker skill procedure correctly and updated shared state documentation appropriately. No issues found.
